flashcards.front: What is line segment clipping in computer graphics?
flashcards.back: It determines which portions of a line segment are visible within a specified rectangular region called a clip window or viewport. It prevents rendering of invisible portions.
A line clipping algorithm that uses 4-bit region codes to classify endpoints as inside or outside the clip window, enabling efficient trivial accept/reject tests.

flashcards.front: What do the 4 bits in Cohen-Sutherland region code represent?
flashcards.back: Top (1000), Bottom (0100), Right (0010), Left (0001) - indicating which side of the clip window the point lies beyond.

flashcards.front: When does Cohen-Sutherland algorithm trivially accept a line segment?
flashcards.back: When both endpoints have region code 0000 (both inside), or when the logical AND of their codes is non-zero (both on same outside region).

flashcards.front: When does Cohen-Sutherland algorithm trivially reject a line segment?
flashcards.back: When the logical AND of the region codes of both endpoints is non-zero, meaning they share a common outside region.
A parametric line clipping algorithm that uses parametric equations and inequality tests to clip line segments against a rectangular window efficiently.

flashcards.front: What is the parametric form of a line segment used in Liang-Barsky?
flashcards.back: x = x1 + t(x2-x1), y = y1 + t(y2-y1) where 0 ≤ t ≤ 1 gives points between endpoints.

flashcards.front: What are the clipping parameters in Liang-Barsky algorithm?
flashcards.back: p1=-(x2-x1), p2=(x2-x1), p3=-(y2-y1), p4=(y2-y1) for left, right, bottom, top edges respectively.

flashcards.front: What is Cyrus-Beck algorithm used for?
flashcards.back: A parametric line clipping algorithm that works with convex clip windows using dot products to determine entry and exit points.

flashcards.front: Define clip window in computer graphics
flashcards.back: A rectangular region defined by minimum and maximum x and y coordinates (xmin, xmax, ymin, ymax) that defines the visible area of the output display.
The region on the output device (screen) where the clip window is mapped and displayed. It is the rectangular area on the monitor.

flashcards.front: How is clipping different from windowing?
flashcards.back: Clipping removes portions outside the window; windowing is the process of mapping world coordinates to screen coordinates for display.
An algorithm for clipping polygons against a rectangular window by processing each edge of the clip window sequentially.

flashcards.front: What is the Cohen-Sutherland vs Liang-Barsky main difference?
flashcards.back: Cohen-Sutherland uses region codes and geometric calculations; Liang-Barsky uses parametric approach with inequality calculations - more efficient for rectangular windows.

flashcards.front: What is trivial acceptance in line clipping?
flashcards.back: When both endpoints of a line segment are inside the clip window (code 0000), the entire line is visible and no clipping is needed.
